name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Houston, Tx
description_short: NACA offers character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and no mortgage insurance, targeting low-to-moderate income and historically underserved communities.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has evolved into a national organization dedicated to closing the racial wealth disparity gap through affordable homeownership. Over 36 years, NACA has developed a mission-driven model focused on advocacy and direct service to underserved communities. The organization has served 3 million people, helped 500,000 homeowners achieve ownership, and made over $20 billion in mortgage commitments.

NACA offers their signature "Best in America Mortgage" featuring zero down payment requirements, zero closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no credit score consideration. Instead, they employ character-based lending that evaluates applicants' circumstances holistically. Current interest rates (as of April 6, 2026) are 5.625% for 30-year fixed, 5.125% for 20-year fixed, and 5% for 15-year fixed mortgages. The organization has originated 75,000 mortgages and provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationwide. Prospective members can attend "Achieve the Dream" multi-day events in various cities to become NACA Qualified and access their services.

What distinguishes NACA is their explicit rejection of traditional credit-scoring underwriting in favor of evaluating character, payment history, and personal circumstances. They combine mortgage lending with community advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors. NACA operates on a mission-driven nonprofit model with volunteer opportunities (NACtivist program) and requires active member participation. Their leadership includes Bruce Marks, founder and CEO, who hosts a podcast about affordable homeownership and fighting predatory lending.

Honestly, NACA's model is highly selective—applicants must attend multi-day in-person events and qualify through their specific character-based evaluation process, which is not a traditional mortgage pathway. While their rates and terms are genuinely competitive and their mission is transparent, the organization's activist stance and community engagement requirements mean they are fundamentally different from conventional mortgage lenders. They are best suited for borrowers who align with their mission, have time to engage in the qualification process, and need alternatives to traditional credit-based lending.
pros: Zero down payment required—no savings barrier to homeownership
Zero closing costs and fees—eliminates thousands in upfront expenses
No mortgage insurance required—saves thousands over loan life
Character-based underwriting ignores credit scores entirely
Below-market fixed interest rates (5.625% 30-year as of April 2026)
Provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally
Community-based approach with volunteer activism opportunities
Transparent mission-driven model with 36 years of track record
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day in-person 'Achieve the Dream' events—significant time commitment
Selective qualification process—not all applicants will be approved despite no credit score requirement
Limited geographic availability—events held only in specific cities on specific dates
Activist involvement expected—members encouraged to participate in campaigns and NACtivist programs
No online application pathway evident—process requires in-person participation and engagement
best_for: Low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers with poor or no credit history
Borrowers from historically underserved communities and communities of color
Applicants willing to engage in community advocacy and mission-aligned activism
Borrowers seeking alternatives to traditional credit-based mortgage underwriting
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers and historically underserved borrowers who have time to attend in-person qualification events and align with their mission-driven, activist approach to affordable homeownership. The main caveat is that NACA is fundamentally a selective, mission-based organization—not a conventional mortgage lender—so qualification requires active participation in their community-based process and may not be appropriate for borrowers seeking a traditional, streamlined online application.
services: Best in America Mortgage with no down payment
Character-based underwriting without credit score consideration
Multi-day Achieve the Dream qualifying events
Member portal for document upload and file access
NACtivist volunteer program and community engagement opportunities
Real estate agent network and contractor partnerships
Lender, servicer, and investor partner network
Advocacy and campaigns against predatory landlords
Podcast education (Breaking Barriers with Bruce Marks)
Testimonial and member success story tracking
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 14425 Torrey Chase Blvd Ste 200, Houston, TX 77014
